reference.title: A New Class of Roche Lobe-filling Hot Subdwarf Binaries
reference.note: Cited as the canonical multi-method WD+sdB analysis used in our ablation. The published_params below were extracted manually from the paper Tables 1-4 and Section 4.

published_params.claim: Orbital period from ZTF light curve and Keck spectroscopy.
Effective temperature of the hot subdwarf primary from SED fit.
Surface gravity of the sdB from atmosphere model.
sdB mass from orbital + atmosphere joint solution.
Unseen white-dwarf companion mass from RV semiamplitude + sdB mass.

key_claims: The system is a Roche-lobe filling hot subdwarf (sdB) plus white dwarf binary with a 39.3-minute orbital period.
The sdB is overfilling its Roche lobe and transferring mass to the unseen WD companion.
The system is a likely AM CVn progenitor.
The orbital decay timescale from gravitational radiation is approximately 39 Myr.
The eclipse depth and shape rule out a main-sequence companion via the Rayleigh-Jeans / ingress test.
The radial-velocity semi-amplitude combined with sdB mass yields a WD mass of 0.545 +/- 0.02 Msun.
